sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: Apply confidence interval ideas to interpretation tasks. Use \( \alpha = 0.05 \) as the default significance level unless stated otherwise.

sections.heading: Key ideas
sections.content:
A confidence interval estimates a population parameter. For a mean:
\[ \bar{x} \pm z_{\alpha/2} \frac{s}{\sqrt{n}} \]
links sample spread to interval width. Sample size \(n = 10\) affects precision.

content.title: Confidence interval template
content.purpose: Check statements about interval interpretation and record reasons.
content.learner_task: Complete both template tables using the confidence interval \( (66.08, 73.92) \) where needed.

materials.template:
### Confidence Interval Template
| Statement | Correct? | Reason |
|----------|----------|--------|
| 95% probability true mean lies in interval | | |
| Method captures mean 95% of time | | |

### Confidence Levels
| Level | Width | Explanation |
|------|-------|-------------|
| 90% | | |
| 95% | | |
| 99% | | |

content.expected_output: Both tables completed with defensible Yes/No judgements and brief reasons.
content.support_note: Focus on frequentist interpretation, not single-interval probability claims.

content.title: Interval comparison scenario
content.purpose: Compare intervals from two study arms.
content.learner_task: Use the scenario table to decide which arm has the narrower interval and whether intervals overlap.
content.expected_output: A short comparison noting overlap and relative width using the table values.
content.support_note: Quote intervals from the table; do not invent new endpoints.

items.question:
p-value = 0.03 at \( \alpha = 0.05 \):
1. Decision?
2. What does p-value represent?
3. Is it practically significant?

sections.heading: Study tips
sections.content:
- Sketch intervals on a number line before writing long explanations.
- Check whether overlap answers the comparison question before calculating extra tests.

limitations: Golden composed-page fixture for post-Sprint-33/34 renderer contract.
